query_normalized: multi-lcb: extending livecodebench to multiple programming languages computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on cross-lingual summarization of natural language (historical fiction and Wikipedia) and does not address the user's specific question regarding the extension of the LiveCodeBench benchmark to multiple programming languages. While both fields involve "languages" and "models," the constructs (natural language summarization vs. code generation benchmarking) and domains (NLP vs. software engineering) are distinct, failing the criteria for same-mechanism evidence or shared empiric
